name: Charlton All Saints
overview: Charlton All Saints is in the Salisbury area of Wiltshire (SP5), and sits in the rural-to-commuter belt around Salisbury. Historic references and parish records show long-established settlement patterns, with modern housing mostly as infill around older cores. Day-to-day life depends on local village facilities plus Salisbury for larger services. The area appeals to buyers who want a quieter setting but still need practical links to the city.
character: Charlton All Saints has a village-scale layout with mixed period and modern homes, a parish-centred feel, and surrounding countryside as a dominant part of daily character.
amenities: Parish/community facilities in Charlton All Saints
Nearby shopping and services in Salisbury
Local walking routes and countryside access

transport.roads: Village roads generally connect to the A36/A30 corridor for Salisbury access.

typically around 1h30-1h40 from Salisbury station services

thingsToDo: Trips to local heritage sites and surrounding countryside

pros: Reasonable access to city services and rail via Salisbury station
cons: Public transport coverage varies by village and time of day
whoItSuits: Best for buyers prioritising village character and countryside access, while accepting regular trips into Salisbury for wider amenities and commuting connections.
